root_text.an3.123:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kusinārāyaṁ viharati baliharaṇe vanasaṇḍe.
root_text.an3.123:2.1: “Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā upanissāya viharati.
root_text.an3.123:2.2: Tamenaṁ gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā upasaṅkamitvā svātanāya bhattena nimanteti.
root_text.an3.123:2.4: So tassā rattiyā accayena pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena tassa gahapatissa vā gahapatiputtassa vā nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkamati; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdati.
root_text.an3.123:2.5: Tamenaṁ so gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena sahatthā santappeti sampavāreti.
root_text.an3.123:3.2: ‘sādhu vata myāyaṁ gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena sahatthā santappeti sampavāretī’ti.
root_text.an3.123:3.4: ‘aho vata māyaṁ gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā āyatimpi evarūpena paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena sahatthā santappeyya sampavāreyyā’ti.
root_text.an3.123:3.5: So taṁ piṇḍapātaṁ gathito mucchito ajjhosanno anādīnavadassāvī anissaraṇapañño paribhuñjati.
root_text.an3.123:3.6: So tattha kāmavitakkampi vitakketi, byāpādavitakkampi vitakketi, vihiṁsāvitakkampi vitakketi.
root_text.an3.123:3.7: Evarūpassāhaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno dinnaṁ na mahapphalanti vadāmi.
root_text.an3.123:4.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā upanissāya viharati.
root_text.an3.123:4.2: Tamenaṁ gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā upasaṅkamitvā svātanāya bhattena nimanteti.
root_text.an3.123:4.4: So tassā rattiyā accayena pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena tassa gahapatissa vā gahapatiputtassa vā nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkamati; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdati.
root_text.an3.123:4.5: Tamenaṁ so gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena sahatthā santappeti sampavāreti.
root_text.an3.123:5.2: ‘sādhu vata myāyaṁ gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena sahatthā santappeti sampavāretī’ti.
root_text.an3.123:5.4: ‘aho vata māyaṁ gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā āyatimpi evarūpena paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena sahatthā santappeyya sampavāreyyā’ti.
root_text.an3.123:5.5: So taṁ piṇḍapātaṁ agathito amucchito anajjhosanno ādīnavadassāvī nissaraṇapañño paribhuñjati.
root_text.an3.123:5.6: So tattha nekkhammavitakkampi vitakketi, abyāpādavitakkampi vitakketi, avihiṁsāvitakkampi vitakketi.
root_text.an3.123:5.7: Evarūpassāhaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno dinnaṁ mahapphalanti vadāmi.

translation_text.an3.123:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kusinārā, in the Forest of Offerings.
translation_text.an3.123:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an3.123:2.1: “Mendicants, take the case of a mendicant living supported by a town or village.
translation_text.an3.123:2.2: A householder or their child approaches and invites them for the next day’s meal.
translation_text.an3.123:2.3: The mendicant accepts if they want.
translation_text.an3.123:2.4: When the night has passed, they robe up in the morning, take their bowl and robe, and approach that householder’s home, where they sit on the seat spread out.
translation_text.an3.123:2.5: The householder or their child serves and satisfies them with their own hands with delicious fresh and cooked foods.
translation_text.an3.123:3.2: ‘It’s so good that this householder serves me with delicious fresh and cooked foods.’
translation_text.an3.123:3.4: ‘I really hope this householder serves me with delicious fresh and cooked foods in the future, too.’
translation_text.an3.123:3.5: They eat that food tied, infatuated, attached, blind to the drawbacks, and not understanding the escape.
translation_text.an3.123:3.6: They think about it with sensual, malicious, or cruel thoughts.
translation_text.an3.123:3.7: A gift to such a mendicant is not very fruitful, I say.
translation_text.an3.123:4.1: Take another case of a mendicant living supported by a town or village.
translation_text.an3.123:4.2: A householder or their child approaches and invites them for the next day’s meal.
translation_text.an3.123:4.3: The mendicant accepts if they want.
translation_text.an3.123:4.4: When the night has passed, they robe up in the morning, take their bowl and robe, and approach that householder’s home, where they sit on the seat spread out.
translation_text.an3.123:4.5: The householder or their child serves and satisfies them with their own hands with delicious fresh and cooked foods.
translation_text.an3.123:5.2: ‘It’s so good that the householder or their child serves and satisfies me with their own hands with delicious fresh and cooked foods.’
translation_text.an3.123:5.4: ‘I really hope this householder serves me with delicious fresh and cooked foods in the future, too.’
translation_text.an3.123:5.5: They eat that almsfood untied, uninfatuated, unattached, seeing the drawback, and understanding the escape.
translation_text.an3.123:5.6: They think about it with thoughts of renunciation, good will, or harmlessness.
translation_text.an3.123:5.7: A gift to such a mendicant is very fruitful, I say.

variant_text.an3.123:3.5: gathito → gadhito (sya-all, km, mr) | ajjhosanno → ajjhāpanno (bj, mr); ajjhopanno (sya-all, pts1ed ṭīkā)
